- Backlog Item ID: BI-260003
- Title: Establish secondary-user personas and GraphQL backend contract
- Persona/Actor: Data manager, developer integrator, and spreadsheet-workflow user
- Value statement: For secondary users and integration partners, establish a stable GraphQL contract and secondary-persona direction so post-MVP extensibility and API reliability are predictable.
- Context: Product definition phase transitioning to backend-contract and data-model commitment.
- Problem statement: Without contract clarity and error-handling boundaries, frontend implementation risks instability and post-MVP integration friction.
- Expected outcome: Team has a versioned backend contract, error-handling baseline, and documented secondary-user roadmap.
- Success metric(s):
  - A3 and B1-B5 are accepted and encoded in formal artifacts.
  - Linked FR/OR/CR requirements exist with verifiable contract behaviors.
- Acceptance criteria (behavior):
  - ADR-260003 is created and accepted.
  - REQ-FR-260005, REQ-FR-260006, REQ-OR-260002, and REQ-CR-260002 are created and linked.
  - Backlog status includes BI-260003 with full traceability.
- Non-goals: Implementing post-MVP secondary-user features or label administration.
- Constraints: Preserve GraphQL contract alignment and avoid adding deferred operations to MVP scope.
- Dependencies: Backend user guide, GraphQL schema introspection.
- Risks/assumptions:
  - Risk: Label querying deferral could surface as user friction if queries are expected to return empty results.
  - Assumption: Backend will maintain schema compatibility across release boundaries as specified.
- Validation approach: Contract tests, error case tests, backwards-compatibility validation.
- Priority score: 97
- Target window: MVP foundation
- Links:
  - Requirement entry: REQ-FR-260005.md, REQ-FR-260006.md, REQ-OR-260002.md, REQ-CR-260002.md
  - ADR: ADR-260003.md
  - ICR: n/a
  - Changeset: n/a

## Done Evidence

This backlog item is marked as Done because the A3 and B1-B5 decisions (except detailed B3 compatibility governance) have been accepted and encoded in ADR-260003 and the linked requirements (REQ-FR-260005, REQ-FR-260006, REQ-OR-260002, REQ-CR-260002). These establish the secondary-user personas and GraphQL backend contract baseline, providing stability for post-MVP extensibility. The detailed B3 compatibility governance was implemented separately in BI-260010. The acceptance criteria have been fully satisfied through the creation and linking of the required ADR and requirement records, with traceability maintained in the backlog status tracker.
